what_i_noticed: Every stock in the engine output shows crash_prob_3m = 2.0% (identical). Combined with 98.8% feature drift (161/163 features), the crash model is effectively dead. The IsotonicRegression calibrator with out_of_bounds='clip' collapses all drifted inputs to its minimum, and np.clip(calibrated, 0.02, 0.98) at crash_model.py:336 masks this by flooring everything at exactly 2%. Secondary findings: walk_forward.py use_purged flag is a no-op (logged but never applied), return_model.py has index alignment bug in per-horizon training loop.
hypothesis: Replacing the hardcoded 0.02 floor with a configurable 0.001 floor + degenerate calibrator detection + base rate fallback will restore meaningful crash probability output. When the calibrator is out-of-distribution, falling back to the historical training base rate (~12%) gives downstream consumers a conservative but useful signal instead of a useless 2%.
what_i_did: 1. Moved crash probability clip bounds from hardcoded values to config['ml']['calibration']. 2. Added degenerate calibrator detection: when >50% of predictions hit the floor, log WARNING and optionally fall back to training base rate. 3. Added OOB detection: log when blended scores fall outside calibrator's training range. 4. Extracted _blend_scores() to eliminate code duplication between predict_proba and _raw_calibrated. 5. Added diagnostics() method and GET /api/crash/diagnostics endpoint. 6. Persisted calibrator input range through save/load (backward compatible). 7. Moved IsotonicRegression y_min/y_max to config. 8. Narrowed broad except block in diagnostics router. 9. Wrote 42 tests covering all new code paths and edge cases.

after.crash_prob_3m_behavior: Degenerate output detected → falls back to ~12% base rate + WARNING logged
after.crash_model_status: GET /api/crash/diagnostics reports healthy/degraded with per-horizon stats

analysis: This fix addresses a real, user-visible problem — every crash probability was stuck at 2.0%, making crash predictions useless across the entire app (signals, stock pages, sectors, portfolio). The base rate fallback is a band-aid: 12% for every stock is better than 2% for every stock, but still not differentiated. The real fix requires retraining the crash model on recent data to reduce the 98.8% feature drift. The diagnostics endpoint is genuinely useful — it makes model health visible to operators and the frontend. The code quality improvements (extracted _blend_scores, config-driven bounds, narrowed exceptions) are solid. Self-critique: diagnostics() calls both _raw_calibrated and predict_proba per horizon, meaning 2x inference cost. Acceptable for a diagnostic endpoint but worth noting. The OOB logging on every predict_proba call could be noisy in production — should consider rate-limiting or only logging once per session.
what_i_would_do_differently: 1. Would have also tackled the walk_forward.py purged CV no-op (Finding #2) — it's a correctness bug that inflates backtest metrics. Only avoided it because the crash floor fix was more user-visible. 2. The OOB info-level logging could flood logs in a high-traffic deployment. Should add a 'logged once' flag or rate-limit it. 3. Could have added a frontend component to surface the diagnostics endpoint (e.g., health badge on the crash page). 4. The base rate fallback is uniform across all stocks — could be improved by using beta-adjusted base rates per ticker.
next_steps: 1. Retrain crash model with recent data to reduce 98.8% feature drift (the actual root cause). 2. Implement purged CV in walk_forward.py (Finding #2 — correctness bug). 3. Fix return_model.py index alignment (Finding #3). 4. Consider per-ticker base rate fallback using beta adjustment. 5. Rate-limit OOB logging in predict_proba.
